response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and backend software engineer with a strong track record in web platforms, developer tooling, and some low-latency systems work — but his background has virtually no overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware-software co-design, deep-learning workload analysis, and GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture, none of which appear in the resume. His embedded systems exposure is minimal and incidental, and while he has touched performance-sensitive backend code (DLang async I/O at Sociomantic), that is categorically different from silicon-level performance modeling for AI hardware.
strengths: 13+ years of professional engineering experience satisfies the BS+8yr educational pathway requirement
Demonstrated interest in performance-sensitive systems (low-latency micro-services at Sociomantic, high-throughput direct-mail pricing at Optilyz)
Broad polyglot background and systems thinking across web, mobile, and embedded domains shows adaptability
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, or any silicon-level performance work — the core technical requirement of the role
No AI/ML background whatsoever; no deep-learning training/inference optimization, GPU workload analysis, or SIMD/MIMD architecture exposure
Current role and entire career trajectory are firmly in software product/web engineering, with no indication of a pivot toward hardware or AI accelerator domains

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP or hardware modeling experience
